findings.finding: matches_any_glob(rel_path, globs: Iterable[str]) admits one-shot generators with no barrier; a reused exhausted generator silently returns False. No current caller at risk (all pass module-level tuples). Consider Sequence[str] or a docstring note.
findings.evidence_quote: Expose the public predicate `matches_any_glob(rel_path: str, globs: Iterable[str]) -> bool`

findings.finding: The mandatory depth-1 synthetic parity test inherits the dual-corpus false-lock: a pre-existing depth-1 file is found via root.glob regardless of whether _matches_scan_glob matches it at depth-1.
findings.evidence_quote: a depth-1 synthetic in-scope file (e.g. `skills/foo.md` against `skills/**/*.md`) is mandatory for every checker whose globs contain a `**` glob

findings.finding: prose/bare-python have no working-tree fallback unlike parity; the uniform 'no collateral damage' claim is asymmetric — parity already read deep files via root.glob (implicitly clean), but prose/bare-python were blind to the 44 newly-in-scope skills/**/*.md files until the fix.
findings.evidence_quote: the corrected matcher surfaces no latent findings in this repo's deep files

findings.finding: At Task 2 the fixed parity_check newly scans tests/test_lint_globs.py (depth-1, in-scope). Safe (no cortex-* refs) but the plan presents the Task1->Task2 dependency as purely mechanical without documenting the changed scan surface.
findings.evidence_quote: Task 2: Route the three importable checkers through `matches_any_glob`, delete old membership calls ... Depends on: [1]

findings.finding: Task 4's 'set an env guard' alternative is a dead end: the script (lines 28-34) has no env-guard branch; adding one requires modifying the script, which Task 3 forbids and which is outside Task 4's new-test-file scope.
findings.evidence_quote: monkeypatch `shutil.which` -> `None`, or set an env guard
